subject: english
chapter_title: A Tale of Valour — Major Somnath Sharma and the Battle of Badgam (Values and Dispositions)

main_sections.title: A Tale of Valour — Major Somnath Sharma and the Battle of Badgam
main_sections.prose_summary: This section is an informational tribute to Major Somnath Sharma, India's first recipient of the Param Vir Chakra. Set against the backdrop of the 1947 Indo-Pakistan conflict, the narrative recounts how Pakistan launched Operation Gulmarg to annex the Kashmir Valley, training around 1000 infiltrators to capture Srinagar airport. In response, D Company of the 4th Battalion, Kumaon Regiment, under Major Somnath Sharma, was airlifted to Srinagar on 31 October 1947. Despite a plastered left hand, Sharma insisted on leading his 90 men, famously declaring 'I know my men better than anyone else.' On 3 November, when the enemy — roughly 500 strong — attacked D Company at Badgam, Sharma held his ground with tenacity, correctly identifying an initial attack as a decoy. He called for air strikes, laid ground panels under enemy fire, filled ammunition magazines for his gunners, and moved fearlessly under devastating fire urging his men to hold. When ordered to withdraw, he responded with the legendary words: 'The enemy is only 50 yards from us. We are heavily outnumbered. We are under devastating fire. I shall not withdraw an inch but will fight to the last man and the last round.' Minutes later, a mortar shell killed him instantly. Despite losing their commander, his men held the position for six more hours before being overrun, buying time for reinforcements to save Srinagar. Major Sharma was awarded the Param Vir Chakra posthumously, the first such recipient. His citation praises his personal courage, leadership, and sacrifice for holding off an overwhelming enemy force. The section also profiles his early life — born in Kangra in 1923, educated at Sherwood College and the Royal Military College Sandhurst, he served with distinction in World War II's Arakan Campaign. The closing pages include his actual Ministry of Defence citation and a photograph of his name etched on the National War Memorial's Honour Wall, underscoring the enduring legacy of his sacrifice at age 24. The 'Let us explore' section introduces twenty-one PVC awardees from Major Sharma (first) to Captain Vikram Batra (last), along with descriptions of other gallantry awards and the role of women in the Indian Armed Forces.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us read + Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Work in pairs. Share your answers with your classmates and teacher. (Before-reading activities about Republic Day Parade, bravery awards, and soldier qualities — p.49)

question_bank.stem: What do you think about the Republic Day Parade on 26 January?
What are the highlights of this parade?
What bravery awards are given on this day, and who receives them?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Work in groups of four and discuss. (Before-reading activities about soldier qualities — p.49)

question_bank.stem: What qualities do you think a soldier should have?
If you had to share a message with a soldier, what would it be?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Arrange the following events in chronological order. The first and the last events have been marked for you. Share your answers with your classmates and teacher. (Let us discuss — p.59)

question_bank.stem: Arrange events in chronological order: (1) Major Somnath Sharma was born in 1923 in Kangra, Himachal Pradesh. (2) Even after losing Major Somnath Sharma, the soldiers fought bravely to save Srinagar for India. (3) The D Company, under the command of Major Somnath Sharma, was flown into Srinagar. (4) Major Somnath Sharma underwent training at the Royal Military College, Sandhurst. (5) Major Somnath Sharma requested air strikes to combat the enemy at Badgam. (6) Major Somnath Sharma was commissioned into the 19th Hyderabad Regiment in 1942. (7) Major Somnath Sharma was killed immediately after a mortar shell hit him. (8) Major Somnath Sharma was the first recipient of the Param Vir Chakra.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the given extracts and answer the questions that follow. (Let us think and reflect, Extract 1 — p.59-60)

question_bank.stem:
What can be inferred about the conditions from the line: 'The air was thick with gunfire...'
A. The gunshots had stopped for a while.
B. There was intense fighting on the battlefield.
C. The invaders were retreating from the fight.
D. The Indian soldiers had achieved victory.
Complete the sentence with a suitable reason. Despite heavy casualties, Major Somnath Sharma continued to lead his men because ________.

Select the correct option for both Assertion (A) and Reason (R): (A) Major Somnath Sharma moved fearlessly through the battlefield, even though he was injured. (R) He believed his courage was important to motivate the soldiers.
A. Both (A) and (R) are true, and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
B. Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
C. (A) is true, but (R) is false.
D. (A) is false, but (R) is true.
Fill in the blank with the correct word from those given in the brackets: Major Somnath Sharma was filling magazines, and encouraging his men to hold their ground despite ________. (injuries/worry)

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the given extracts and answer the questions that follow. (Let us think and reflect, Extract 2 — p.60-61)

question_bank.stem:
What can be inferred about Somnath Sharma's early influences on his career?
A. He was primarily influenced by his father's achievements in the military.
B. His inspiration came solely from the tales of the Mahabharata.
C. His family and heritage inspired his bravery and sense of duty.
D. He decided to join the military out of necessity rather than passion.
Identify whether the following statement is true or false: Somnath Sharma belonged to a wealthy family.
What does the phrase 'served the nation with distinction' tell us about Major General Amar Nath Sharma?

Replace the underlined phrase with a word from the extract that has a similar meaning: My family and I visited a popular hill station and stayed in an old but visually charming house surrounded by trees.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Answer the following questions. (Let us think and reflect, Part II — p.61)

question_bank.stem: 'Major Somnath's legacy of courage and devotion to the nation remains forever alive, forever alive…' What is the significance of the repetition in this line?
Why do you think Major Somnath's leadership was critical during the Battle of Badgam?
What were Major Somnath's last words? What do they reflect about his sense of duty?
What might have been the outcome of the Battle of Badgam if Major Somnath and his company had not held their ground?
Why do you think Major Somnath's story continues to inspire soldiers and civilians today?
How can the values demonstrated by Major Somnath Sharma—courage, duty, and self-sacrifice—be applied in our daily lives?

listening.transcript_text: Hello, everyone! Let me tell you about the Param Vir Chakra. It is one of the most prestigious awards in India and signifies the 'Wheel of the Ultimate Brave.' This award is granted for 'exemplary bravery in the presence of the enemy.' The medal was instituted as a Gallantry Medal with retrospective effect from 15 August 1947 by Dr. Rajendra Prasad, the then President of India. The medal was designed by Mrs. Savitri Khanolkar, who drew inspiration from Sage Dadhichi, a Vedic rishi. The Param Vir Chakra is cast in bronze. At its centre, on a raised circle, is the State Emblem of India. Surrounding it are four replicas of Indra's Vajra, lined with lotus flowers.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to a talk about the Param Vir Chakra. As you listen, complete the sentences by filling in the blanks with one or two exact words or phrases you hear. (p.66)

question_bank.stem: The Param Vir Chakra signifies the 'Wheel of the Ultimate ________'.
The Param Vir Chakra is awarded to someone who shows exemplary bravery while fighting with the ________.
The Param Vir Chakra was introduced as a Gallantry Medal in 1947 by the ________.
The design of the Param Vir Chakra is inspired by Sage Dadhichi, who was a ________.
The state emblem in the centre of the Param Vir Chakra is surrounded by the Vajra and ________.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: As the citizens of a nation, we all must fulfil our duty. In groups of 4–5, speak about how you propose to serve your nation. You can choose from the themes: Saving the environment / Health and well-being / Helping others / Righteous conduct. Use the sentence prompts provided to organise what you speak about. (p.66-67)

A letter of enquiry is a formal letter written to seek detailed information about something the writer wants to know. Now, write a letter of enquiry. Your school wishes to organise a visit to a Memorial in your state/region. Write the letter to the incharge of that memorial requesting for information regarding available dates for visits, the duration of the tour, any entry fees, and guidelines for student groups. Follow the given layout and use the cues provided. (p.67-68)

question_bank.stem: Write a formal letter of enquiry to a memorial incharge requesting information about: (a) available dates for school visits, (b) duration of the tour, (c) entry fees, and (d) guidelines for student groups. Use the layout and prompts provided.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Match the expressions from the text in Column 1 with the most suitable situations given in Column 2: fight to the last man / foil the plan / deliver a blow / hold their ground. Then complete sentences A–D using the expressions from Column 1. (p.62)

question_bank.stem: Match: 1. Fight to the last man — (i)/(ii)/(iii)/(iv); 2. Foil the plan; 3. Deliver a blow; 4. Hold their ground.
question_bank.table:
Expression|Situation
Fight to the last man|(i) A sports team scores a last-minute goal, shaking the opponent's confidence.
Foil the plan|(ii) The soldiers fought valiantly until each one laid down his life.
Deliver a blow|(iii) Soldiers stay in their position and fight back, even though the enemy is stronger.
Hold their ground|(iv) A spy disrupts the enemy's efforts to invade a country by intercepting their communications.

question_bank.stem:
Complete sentences using expressions from Column 1:
A. The detective managed to ________ by finding the hidden evidence before the culprit could get rid of it.
B. The company's new advertising campaign was meant to ________ to their competitors' sales.
C. The hikers chose to ________ despite the heavy rain, refusing to turn back on their journey.
D. Despite being heavily outnumbered, the soldiers vowed to defend their homeland and ________, refusing to surrender to the enemy forces.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Match the collective nouns for armed forces in Column 1 to their meanings in Column 2 (regiment / squadron / fleet / battalion). Then fill in the blanks suitably with words from Column 1. (p.62-63)

question_bank.stem:
Match collective nouns:
1. regiment — (i)/(ii)/(iii)/(iv); 2. squadron; 3. fleet; 4. battalion.
question_bank.table:
Collective Noun|Meaning
regiment|(i) a large body of troops ready for battle, especially an infantry unit
squadron|(ii) a permanent unit of an army typically commanded by a lieutenant colonel/colonel and divided into several companies
fleet|(iii) a small unit, typically comprising 12–24 aircrafts and their crews used in air force, naval, or army aviation services
battalion|(iv) a group of ships sailing together, engaged in the same activity, or under the same ownership

question_bank.stem:
Fill in the blanks with words from Column 1:
A. The commanding officer addressed the entire ________ of soldiers during the morning assembly.
B. The admiral ordered the entire ________ to set sail for battle.
C. The ________ of infantry advanced slowly through the dense forest.
D. A ________ of fighter jets was sent to intercept the enemy planes.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the words and fill in the blanks with the correct words by taking clues from the meanings: overwhelm / overthrow / overrun. Then create a list of at least five other verbs that begin with 'over' and frame sentences of your own. (p.64)

question_bank.stem:
Fill in the blanks:
1. To take and seize control: ________
2. To invade or occupy an area in large numbers: ________
3. To flood or overpower someone or something emotionally: ________
Then create a list of at least five other verbs that begin with 'over' and frame sentences.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Study noun-verb pairs where 'c' is used for nouns and 's' for verbs: advice/advise, practice/practise, device/devise, council/counsel. Fill in the blanks correctly with the words given in the brackets. Then note the British-vs-American spelling of 'defence/defense'. (p.64)

question_bank.stem:
Fill in the blanks with the correct form (noun 'c' / verb 's'):
1. The teacher's (i) ________ is always helpful, and she continues to (ii) ________ students on their career choices. (advice/advise)
2. The musician believes that daily (i) ________ helps a lot, so she is always ready to (ii) ________ her harmonium lessons. (practice/practise)
3. The scientist uses a strange (i) ________ for experiments and plans to (ii) ________ new methods for his analysis. (device/devise)
4. The village (i) ________ met to discuss issues and decided to (ii) ________ the villagers on important matters. (council/counsel)

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read highlighted sentences showing Subject-Verb Agreement. Fill in the blanks for 10 sentences by choosing the correct verb given within brackets. Then read the passage and correct the incorrect usage of verbs. (p.65-66)

question_bank.stem:
Fill in the blanks (Subject-Verb Agreement):
1. The bouquet of flowers ________ (is/are) beautiful.
2. Neither the teacher nor the students ________ (was/were) aware of the change in the schedule.
3. Each of the participants ________ (has/have) submitted their projects on time.
4. The data collected by the researchers ________ (shows/show) significant trends.
5. One of the players ________ (was/were) injured during the match.
6. Either the children or their guardian ________ (has/have) to attend the meeting.
7. A pair of shoes ________ (is/are) missing from the rack.
8. Neither of the statements ________ (is/are) true.
9. The members of the jury ________ (has deliver/have delivered) their verdict to the court.
10. Mathematics ________ (is/are) an interesting subject for many students.

Read the passage and correct the incorrect usage of verbs (one example is done): 'Each soldier salute with pride while the commander and his assistants inspects the parade. The sound of marching feet echo through the air as a group of cadets prepare for their drill. Meanwhile, a convoy of military vehicles pass by, drawing cheers from the gathered crowd.'

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Out of twenty-one PVC awardees, Major Somnath Sharma was the first recipient of the PVC, and Captain Vikram Batra was the last. Read the passage about Captain Vikram Batra. Find out more about gallantry awards. Then research the key roles of women in the Indian Armed Forces and share findings with classmates and teacher. (p.68-69)

question_bank.stem: Find out more about gallantry awards in India (Param Vir Chakra, Maha Vir Chakra, Vir Chakra). Share your findings with classmates and teacher.

The Indian government has been steadfast in its initiatives to boost the representation of women in the Indian Armed Forces. Find out the key roles of women in the Indian Army. Share the information with your classmates and teacher.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read about Birsa Munda: A Saga of Courage and Sacrifice. Make a list of the patriotic songs in any language, including songs from your region. Share the songs with your classmates and teacher. (p.69)

question_bank.stem: Make a list of patriotic songs in any language, including songs from your region. Share the songs with your classmates and teacher.
